@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "caddy-3896-frequent-hangs-when-using-http-2-push",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "caddy: Frequent hangs when using http/2 push",
+    "description": "Frequent hangs when using http/2 push. This issue affects 8+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify caddy troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your caddy installation:\n```bash\ncaddy version\ncaddy validate --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile\n```\nLook for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review caddy configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant caddy configuration:\nMy team and I use Caddy as a reverse proxy, and we rely on HTTP/2 Push a lot. We've started with v2.2.1, on certain configurations we experienced random hangs."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Frequent hangs when using http/2 push",
+        "description": "This issue was caused by a Go standard library bug (golang/go#42534) affecting HTTP/2 server push. Chrome has since dropped HTTP/2 push support entirely.\n\nTo resolve the hangs, remove any `push` directives from your Caddyfile:\n```\n# Before (causes hangs):\nexample.com {\n  push\n  reverse_proxy backend:8080\n}\n\n# After (remove push directive):\nexample.com {\n  reverse_proxy backend:8080\n}\n```\nHTTP/2 push is no longer supported by major browsers and should not be used."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Frequent hangs when using http/2 push is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\nTest caddy to confirm the issue is resolved.\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The root cause of the HTTP/2 push hangs was traced to a Go standard library bug (golang/go#42534) in the HTTP/2 server push implementation. The issue was ultimately closed as won't-fix because Chrome dropped support for HTTP/2 server push entirely, making the feature obsolete. Users experiencing this issue should remove HTTP/2 push directives from their Caddy configuration.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
